observations.recommendation: We recommended the City Mayor to (a) direct the CA to (i) prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the noted reconciling items that require immediate adjustment in the books for an accurate reporting of CIB, LCCA account balance; and (ii) promptly notify the bank(s) of any noted errors in their posting of cash transactions; (b) require the CT and CA to regularly reconcile their cash records for the timely correction of errors; and (c) direct the CT to immediately effect the appropriate adjustments in the cashbook.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to (a) require the AOs to strictly observe the prescribed periods in the liquidation of CAs; (b) require the CA to (i) refrain from granting multiple CAs to AOs to prevent the accumulation of their CAs and risk of possible use other than the intended purpose; and (ii) record the CAs in the books in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs; (c) refrain from granting CAs to AOs with no fidelity bond; and (d) strictly adhere to the existing law, rules and regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended the City Mayor to (a) instruct the CAO and CGSO, City Assessor’s Office, to gather all records of real properties  consisting of several parcels of land with TCTs, Deed of Sale in favor of the City and Tax Declaration of Property (TDPs) which were not recognized in the books, and facilitate the immediate registration and titling of the lots in the name of the City for their proper recognition in the books of accounts; (b) recognize and take action on the need to revise the Schedule of Market Values through City Ordinance to reflect the appropriate current market value of real properties; (c) direct the CAO to (i) make the necessary adjustments on the overvaluation of Road Networks account and understatement of Power Supply and Other PPE accounts; and (ii) recognize depreciation of PPE accounts in accordance with existing rules and regulations; (d) direct the CGSO to exclude from the RPCPPE the items below the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00 and reconcile these with the CAO records; (e) consider providing the CGSO and CAO with adequate manpower and training to accomplish their tasks in the inventory taking, maintenance of PPELCs, and reconciliation of PPE book balances and physical inventory report; and (f) implement the measures under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 or the Guidelines and Procedures in the Conduct of PPE, Recognition of PPE Items Found at the Station, and Disposition for Non-Existing/Missing PPE items, for the One-time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies.

We recommended the City Mayor to (a) formalize the designation/composition of the IC and give specific instructions to formulate and employ a strategy in conducting annual physical count; (b) require the CGSO Chief to conduct annual inventory of local roads under the LGU's jurisdiction and come-up with the Local Road Inventory and Road Map and Local Road Network Property Card for all roads under the City’s jurisdiction; (c) instruct the CA to maintain and reconcile the Registry of Public Infrastructure with CGSO and disclose information on Road Networks in the Notes to FS; and (d) require the process owners of LRAMS to strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended the City Mayor to require the (a) City Budget Officer to explain the issuance of another ObR No. 101-20-09-4926 in the amount of ₱5,251,743.58 while the same was already included in the ₱15,000,000.00 obligated under ObR No. 101-10-02-1765; (b) the CA to (i) make necessary adjusting entry in the recording of the Building account; (ii) record progress payments as Construction in Progress in accordance with  Section 50, Chapter 3 of the  Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1; and (iii) recognize Accounts Payable or any applicable liability account for every incurrence of liability; (c) the CA and the City Engineer to submit the requisite supporting documents of the payment made on the project to satisfy the documentary requirements for  the conduct of auditorial, legal and technical evaluation  of the payment of project accomplishments.

We recommended the City Mayor to require the (a) City Budget Officer to prepare the  ALOBS/obligation of funds  based on perfected contract/PO; (b) BAC to (i)  post the invitation to bid in the PhilGEPS website; (ii) furnish the Audit Team with  a copy of  the contract agreement and all documents forming  part of the contract such as the  Bidding Documents; Eligibility requirements, Technical and Financial Proposals, Performance Security; Notice of Award of Contract; and Other  supporting documents  for proper review and audit; and (iii)  attend procurement training or capacity development program to strengthen and promote their professionalization; (c) City General Services Officer to submit to the Audit Team the (i) approved contracts or POs and their supporting documents within five working days upon their approval to facilitate timely review and evaluation thereof; and (ii) IARs within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of deliveries to facilitate timely inspection; (d) CA to ensure that DVs are properly supported with complete and relevant documents prior to payment; and (e) CITO and BAC to submit all the required vital documents to facilitate conduct of Information Systems Audit.

We recommended the City Mayor to (a) instruct the CA to (i) transfer and record the LGSF-AC funds received by the City in the TF account; and (ii) maintain a separate subsidiary ledger for every projects under the LGSF; (b) require the CPDO and CA to prepare and submit the quarterly reports on fund utilization and comply with the posting and requirements  copy furnished the Audit Team; (c) direct the CT and CA to immediately revert to the National Treasury the unutilized balance of the LGSF-AC of ₱3,753,523.97; (d) fully capacitate the CPDO by providing adequate technical support in order to facilitate the implementation of their PPAs; and (e) maximize the utilization of LGSF and ensure that the programmed PPAs are well-planned and implementation ready.

We recommended the City Mayor to (a) direct the City Budget Officer to obligate funds based on the contract/purchase order price; (b) as the Head of the Procuring Entity, ensure that the programmed PPAs are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready; and (c) instruct the CAO to (i) refrain from recognizing Accounts Payable for goods or services that are not yet delivered; (ii) ensure that the procurements are supported with complete documentation prior to payment; (iii) cause the immediate submission of the paid DV on the purchase of ambulance supplies amounting to ₱453,268.80; and (iv) strictly adhere to the submission of required documents and financial reports within ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

We recommended the City Mayor to (a) direct the City Engineer (CE), Chief, City Planning and all implementing officials to (i) continue upholding the City’s mandate in promoting local development through the full implementation of all development projects proposed to be undertaken during the budget year to attain the objective of providing timely socio-economic benefits to its constituents; and (ii) to require the concerned project engineers, officers and employees to be prudent in overseeing the timely implementation of PPAs under their responsibility; and (b) require the CBO Chief to (i) take-up obligations based on the contract cost, instead of the ABC or PR cost estimate; and (ii) to perform monthly reconciliation of records with the CA to reflect the accurate balance of obligation at a given period.

We recommended the City Mayor to require the (a) BAC to (i) monitor the submission of PMPP by various Offices to facilitate the preparation and submission of the APP to the Audit Team and other regulatory agencies; (ii) submit the required documentation on all procurements made through public bidding and the alternative modes of procurement; (iii) comply with the posting and reporting requirements in the implementation of projects; and (iv) observe the earliest possible time in the  conduct of public bidding, attendance of observers, training of BAC; and (b) City Engineer to perform detailed engineering, surveys and designs on infrastructure projects in accordance with the standards and specifications prescribed by the City Mayor prior to conduct of bidding.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to (a) submit an explanation for  the utilization of the Special Trust Fund without the benefit of an approved appropriation ordinance and supplemental budget; (b) instruct the CAO to (i) disclose the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) in the Notes to Financial Statements using the format prescribed under Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; (ii) maintain a RAO for the LDRRMF; (iii) adjust the balances of TL-DRRMF and Transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds accounts in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002; and (iv) prepare and submit to the Audit Team a report on sources and utilization of the LDRRMF on a monthly basis; and (c) instruct the CBO to prepare a reliable RAO.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to (a) require the CT, CA and CBO Chief to provide the detailed list of CY 2020 unobligated appropriations that were regarded as available funds under Supplemental Budget No. 3,  otherwise submit a justifiable explanation for the noted deficiency; (b) require the City Human Resource Management Officer thru the CAO to submit to the Audit Team (i) a copy of DTRs/Service reports of personnel who received the COVID-19 hazard pay; (ii) a copy of the accomplishment reports of Job Order employees who received the COVID-19 Hazard Pay; and (iii) a copy of the Office Order to personnel required to physically report for work during the ECQ/MECQ stating the dates and purpose; (c) instruct the CA (i) to cause the immediate submission of the DVs and LRs on the cash advances  totaling  ₱4,669,011.33; and (ii) to strictly comply with the submission of requisite documents and financial reports within ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to (a) instruct the CGSO Chief to refund the total amount of ₱262,817.00 and to refrain from claiming BAC honoraria in accordance with existing regulations; (b) require the CA to (i) refrain from paying honoraria to personnel already assigned in the procuring unit; (ii) refrain from granting honoraria to BAC Committee on procurements made other than competitive bidding; (iii) ensure that payments of honoraria are supported with complete and proper documentation; and (c) direct the BAC members to (i) refund the total amount of ₱1,005,021.00 for inappropriate claim of honoraria from projects procured through small value procurement; (ii) specify the projects subject to the opening of bids in attendance sheets; and (iii) refrain from claiming honoraria on projects that are procured through alternative mode of procurement.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) as the chairman of the LSB authorize the payments of DepEd Malolos teaching and non-teaching personnel’s actual communication expense to an amount  not exceeding ₱300.00 per month per claimant; (b) require the City accountant to ensure that the supporting documents for the claims are complete before certifying in the DV; and (c) require the Schools Division Superintendent (SDS) to submit (i) Certification by the authorized claimant, stating that the actual communication expenses were incurred in the performance of official duties and responsibilities; and (ii) proof of purchase/payment such as the copy of official receipt for the purchase of prepaid card or payment of postpaid line or internet subscription under the name of the claimant .

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to (a) require the City Planning and Development Officer and City Budget Officer to ensure that the total LGU budget appropriations under the Annual Budget shall include the activities supporting GAD PPAs; (b) capacitate the GFPS and concerned officials with the mechanisms and processes in mainstreaming GAD perspective in the LGU through trainings, conferences, symposia administered by the PCW, DILG or other NGAs directly involved in GAD concerns; and (c) instruct the GFPS to (i) continue to strive for the full implementation of GAD-related PPAs by utilizing the whole amount set aside for the intended  purposes so that the desired outcomes are attained and benefits are enjoyed to the fullest by the intended beneficiaries; and (ii) exert efforts to come up with a database on sex-disaggregated data or perform gender analysis to ensure that allocation of funds on GAD-related programs, projects and activities  (PPAs)  are not based on incorrect assumptions and stereotypes.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to direct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to (i) continuously observe the regulations and laws governing the withholding of taxes and accordingly improve the remittances of the same to the BIR; and (ii) establish coordination to facilitate processing of documents necessary to support the completeness of remittance, recording of transactions and timely transmission of DVs to the Offices concerned to facilitate the immediate remittance of withheld taxes to the BIR.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed that the City Accountant, the City Treasurer and the Authorized Officer (AAO) from the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) continue to deduct the HDMF monthly premiums due from employees and ensure the remittance thereof to the HDMF within the reglementary period set by the said law.

We recommended and the City Mayor concurred that the City Accountant, the City Treasurer and the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) from HRMO continue to ensure the monthly withholding and timely remittance of premiums due to the PHIC by the City Treasurer.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to direct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to continuously observe the regulations and laws governing the withholding of premium contributions and loan amortizations and accordingly improve compliance in the remittances of the same to GSIS.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to require (a) the City Accountant and City Treasurer to (i) submit the 1,838 DVs and their supporting documents to the  Audit Team; and  (ii) strictly comply with the timely rendition of accounts to the Audit Team so as not to delay the conduct of verification and post-audit activities; (b) City Accountant to  observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports such as Monthly Reports, Financial Statements and BRS to the Audit Team; (c) all concerned accountable officers to submit on time all financial documents and reports of  their respective transactions for each month to the CAO for the preparation of the financial reports; and (d) impose sanctions against the accountable officers who continuously fail  to abide by the timely submission of documents and reports, as warranted.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to require the City General Services Officer to submit to the Audit Team the (a) approved contracts or POs and their supporting documents within five working days upon their approval to facilitate timely review and evaluation thereof; and (b) IARs within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance to facilitate the inspection or validation of deliveries.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the (a) persons liable to cause the settlement of the disallowances deemed final and executory; and (b) the City Accountant to (i) record in the books of accounts the issuance and settlements  of the disallowances and charges that have become final and executory as contained in the NFD; and (ii) maintain and properly update the subsidiary ledgers/records for each official/employee determined to be liable/responsible for the amount disallowed/charged/suspended.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the City Accountant to

(a) prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the staled and cancelled checks; and

(b) on a regular basis, reconcile the Cash-in-Bank balances with the records of the City Treasurer to ensure that all transactions affecting the cash accounts are captured in the CAO records.

We recommended that the LCE

issue designation to field collectors to act as an accountable or collecting officers; and

(b) require the Manager of the CEEDO to

(i)    maintain a logbook and file copies of RISs of issued accountable forms to control and monitor the booklets of accountable forms issued to custodians;

(ii) check that the field collectors are officially designated to perform collection functions and handle accountability for accountable forms; and 

(iii) verify regularly the statement of accountability for accountable forms of the custodian and each field collector as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the ending balances on hand for control and monitoring purposes.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to

(a) consider providing the CGSO and CAO with adequate manpower and training to accomplish their tasks in the inventory taking, maintenance of PPELC, and reconciliation of PPE book balances and physical inventory report;

(b) require the City General Services Officer, City Accountant, City Assessor, and City Legal Officer to coordinate their efforts in the titling and recognition in the books of the parcels of land acquired by the CGM through purchase or donation based on their fair value or acquisition cost; and

(c) instruct the City Accountant to

(i) use the Road Networks and Water Supply System accounts in recognizing the cost of the road network account on road lots donated by private individuals; and 

(ii) recognize depreciation of PPE accounts in accordance with Section 262 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to require the

(a) City Treasurer and the City Assessor to combine their efforts in cleansing the data base of the RPT System to generate a reliable assessment roll;

(b) City Treasurer to furnish a list to the City Accountant of active RPT taxpayers and amount of collectibles to serve as basis in the setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year; and


(c) City Accountant to

(i) recognize in the books the amount of RPT/SET collectibles for the year based on the submitted list of RPT/SET receivables by the City Treasurer; and 


(ii) follow the prescribed accounting entries in the recording, distribution and payment of the barangay’s share from the RPT income.
observations.managementAction: Cleansing of the RPT database is still in progress.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE is amenable to require

(a) the City Accountant to 

(i) record the receipt of cash donations for calamity to account TL-DRRMF;

(ii) maintain Registry of Appropriations for the DRRMF to monitor actual expenditures charged to the fund;

(iii) transfer the remaining unexpended balance of QRF and MOOE for CYs 2019 and 2020 amounting to ₱12,087,034.41 and ₱5,363,639.20, respectively, to the TL-LDRRMF account; and 

(iv) revert to the GF the unexpended/unobligated balance of CYs 2014 and 2015 DRRMF aged more than five years of ₱64,281,171.14; and

(b) City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to

(i) strictly comply with the submission of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team for timely verification thereof; and

(ii) prepare the report in accordance with the format prescribed with emphasis on the inclusion of balances of previous years’ appropriation transferred to the STF and the utilization thereof.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the 

(a) BAC to

(i) consolidate the PPMPs into APP;

(ii) post the NOA, PO and contract in the website of GPPB and the PE, and any conspicuous place in the premises of the PE for transparency purposes; and 

(iii) obtain price quotation/bids/final offers from at least three invited suppliers, in case of Negotiated Procurement for Emergency Cases;

(b) City Accountant to

(i) require contractors to submit the mandatory documentary requirements for procurement transactions; and


(ii) prepare journal entry vouchers (JEVs) to correct the balance of Guaranty Deposits and total of Rent Expenses account; and 

(c) BAC, City General Service Officer and City Accountant to require contractors to submit the OSS, stipulate the exact specifications of goods purchased and include the terms and conditions in the contracts/PO.

We recommended and the LCE is amenable to require the

(a) Supply/Property Officer under the CGSO to

(i) take responsibility for the acceptance of all in-kind donations;

(ii) collate all the acknowledgement receipts, proofs of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries; and

(iii) take an inventory of remaining undistributed items and prepare a report thereon copy furnished the Audit Team;

(b) City Accountant to

(i) disclose the inventory of remaining undistributed items of in-kind donations in the Notes to Financial Statements; and

(ii) record the procurement of goods under account Welfare Goods for Distribution and recognize as Welfare Goods Expenses or Donations only upon issuance of the inventories; and

(c) City General Services Officer to submit to the CAO the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) accompanied by the RIS on a monthly basis for drawing a JEV to record the issuances made.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to require the

(a) City Human Resources and Management Officer to submit a copy of the Minutes of Meeting directing the Department Heads to do the scheduling of employees who reported to duty during the pandemic;

(b) Department Heads to submit the schedule of employees required to work during the pandemic stating the dates and purpose as directed by the LCE; and


(c) City Accountant to

(i) correct the deficiencies noted in the review of payrolls and attachments and effect corrections as needed, prior to payment; and

(ii) submit to the Audit Team the liquidation reports for the cash advances for the Hazard Pay totaling ₱15,269,530.78.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to require the City Accountant and City Treasurer to

(a) submit to the Audit Team the liquidation reports for the cash advances for the SRA; and

(b) City Accountant to

(i) submit to the Audit Team the liquidation reports on all cash advances on a monthly basis and as soon as the purposes for which they were given have been served; and

(ii) use the CDJ to record the disbursements made by the accountable officer.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the

(a) City of Malolos Development Council (CMDC) to set a realistic time-frame for the implementation of the identified priority development PPAs; and

(b) BAC and City Engineer to

(i) strictly comply with the posting of information to notify the public of the on-going projects to promote transparency and accountability; and

(ii) submit to the Audit Team the contracts and their supporting documents consisting of the legal and technical eligibility documents of the Contractors;

(c) BAC to post for information purposes, the NOA, contract, PO including Notice to Proceed (NTP) if necessary, in the PhilGEPS website, the website of the Procuring Entity concerned, if available, and at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose; and

(d) City Engineer to perform detailed engineering investigations, surveys and designs in accordance with the standards and specifications prescribed by the LCE prior to the bidding and awarding of any contract for Infrastructure Projects.

We recommended that the LCE require the

(a) City Planning and Development Officer and City Budget Officer to ensure that the total LGU budget appropriations under the Annual Budget shall correspond to the activities supporting GAD PPAs; and

(b) the GFPS

(i) perform its functions as intended for gender mainstreaming in the CGM’s PPAs;

(ii) strive for the full implementation of GAD-related PPAs by utilizing the whole amount set aside for the intended purposes so that the desired outcomes are attained and benefits are enjoyed to the fullest by the intended beneficiaries; and

(iii) conduct capacity development on gender mainstreaming, gender-responsive planning, budgeting, gender analysis and gender assessment, development and utilization of GAD tools.

We recommended and the LCE is amendable to require

(a) the City Accountant adhere with the provisions of RA No. 7916 to protect the preferential rights of the contractors;

(b) the Head of the MRF to monitor the quantity per cubic meter and weight of residual wastes hauled by contractors to ensure that payments made correspond to the services received; and

(c) submit the Ten-year SWMP for approval to the NSWMC and subsequently, implement SWM PPAs as stated therein.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to

(a) continuously observe the regulations and laws governing the withholding of taxes, premium contributions, etc. and accordingly improve the remittances of the same to the respective government agencies concerned, the BIR, GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG; and

(b) establish coordination to facilitate processing of documents necessary to support the completeness of remittance, recording of transactions and timely transmission of DVs to the Offices concerned to facilitate the immediate remittance of the same to the concerned agencies.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to require the City Accountant to 

(a) adjust the distributed 30% share to barangays; and 

(b) prepare an ARPT Collection to support an accurate distribution of RPT shares to augment the scarce resources of the component small barangays.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the City Accountant to require 

(a) the concerned national government officials, members of the BAC, TWG and the consultant so submit the documentary requirements for the payments and refrain from paying honoraria with deficient documentation; and

(b) recognize payments for honoraria to the consultant, students and madrasah instructor to their appropriate expense accounts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require

(a) the City Accountant and City Treasurer to

(i) submit the 8,658 DVs and their supporting documents to the Audit Team; and 

(ii) strictly comply with the timely rendition of accounts to the Audit Team so as not to delay the conduct of verification and post-audit activities;

(b) City Accountant to observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports such as Monthly Reports, Financial Statements and BRS to the Audit Team;

(c) all concerned accountable officers to submit on time all financial documents and reports of their respective transactions for each month to the CAO for the preparation of the financial reports; and 

(d) impose sanctions against the accountable officers who continuously fail to abide with the timely submission of documents and reports, as warranted.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the City General Services Officer to submit to the Audit Team the

(a) approved contracts or POs and their supporting documents within five working days upon their approval to facilitate timely review and evaluation thereof; and

(b) IARs within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance to facilitate the inspection or validation of deliveries.

We recommended that the LCE require the

(a) persons liable to cause the settlement of the disallowances deemed final and executory; and

(b) the City Accountant to

(i) record in the books of accounts the issuance and settlements  of the disallowances and charges that have become final and executory as contained in the NFD; and (ii) maintain and properly update the subsidiary ledgers/records for each official/employee determined to be liable/responsible for the amount disallowed/charged/suspended.
observations.managementAction:
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) issued Memorandum Nos. 2021-57 to2021-74 to the concerned/responsible CGM officers and employees in order to ensure that the 18 Observations with 54 recommendations are addressed.

The Memo provides that the concerned departments are ordered to submit an accomplishment report and proof of compliance on the matter on or before the 10th of July 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE

ensure that the Offices of the GSO and CAO are adequately manned and trained in order that they could substantially accomplish their tasks in the recording, documentation, monitoring, inventory taking, reporting, and reconciliation of PPE records;

(b) require the GSO, CAO, City Assessors Officer, and City Legal Office to further exert their coordinated efforts in ensuring that the parcels of land acquired by the City through purchase or donation are titled and recognized  in the books based on the fair value or acquisition cost to reflect the true financial condition of the City;

(c) require the City Engineering Office to forward a copy of the inspection and acceptance documents on completed public infrastructure projects  for listing at the GSO ;  and

(d)  require the CAO to effect the necessary adjustments on the
(i) Land, Road Networks, and depreciation accounts; and
(ii)   derecognition of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the LCE require  the

(a) CTO and the City Assessor to combine their efforts to cleanse the data base of the RPT System to generate a reliable assessment roll;

(b) CTO to provide a list to the CAO of active RPT taxpayers and amount collectible to serve as basis in the setting-up of RPT/SET Receivable at the beginning of the year, pending cleansing of Assessor’s records; and

(c) CAO, based on the submitted list of RPT/SET receivables by the CTO, recognize in the books the amount of RPT/SET collectibles for the year, and follow the prescribed accounting entries in the distribution and payment of barangay’s RPT share.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the LCE

(a) require the City Treasurer and City Accountant to

(i) facilitate the return of the abandoned NALGU funds totaling ₱4,849,505.00 to the account of the National Treasury; and



          (ii) revert to the General Fund the cash donation amounting to ₱100,000.00, and other identified supposed income totaling ₱3,181,734.92;

(b) require the City Accountant to 

          (i) retrieve the records, if possible, to effect the return of the unexpended balances of trust funds to source agencies; and 

          (ii)  maintain a detailed subsidiary records  for trust funds for reference and proper identification; and

(c)  ensure that the 20% share from fire code fees is to be appropriated exclusively for the operations and maintenance of local fire station.

We recommended that the City Accountant

(a) adjust the distributed 30% share to barangays in the first and second quarter of 2019; and

(b) henceforth, prepare an Abstract of RPT Collection to support an accurate distribution of RPT shares to augment the scarce resources of the component small barangays.

We recommended that the LCE direct the City Social Welfare and Development Office to formulate measures/written policies/guidelines, for the approval of the Sangguniang Panglungsod, as to the qualification, kind/type of financial assistance, the circumstances and the amount of financial assistance to be given to requesting parties to maximize the utilization of the resources of the City thus benefiting a greater number of constituents, particularly those less privileged individuals.

We recommended that the LCE direct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to 

(a) continuously observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding of taxes, premium contributions, et al. and accordingly improve remittances of the same to the respective government agencies concerned, the BIR, GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG;  

(b)  establish coordination to facilitate processing of documents necessary to support the completeness of remittance, recording of transactions and timely transmission of disbursement vouchers to the respective Offices concerned; and 

(c)  exert extra effort in identifying the nature of the previous years’ balances due to GSIS and PhilHealth and make the necessary adjustment thereon to facilitate the immediate remittance of the same to the concerned agencies.

We recommended that  the LCE  

(a) require the City Accountant and City Treasurer to 

(i) strictly comply with the timely rendition of accounts and financial reports to the COA  Team  so as not to delay the conduct of verification and post-audit activities; and 

(ii) observe the due  dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team;  

(b) require all concerned accountable officers to submit on time all financial documents and reports of  their respective transactions for each month for the preparation of the financial reports;  and 

(c) impose sanctions against the accountable officers who continuously fail  to abide with the timely submission of documents and reports, as warranted.

We recommended  that the LCE  require the City General Services Officer (CGSO) to submit 

(a) the approved contracts or purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Audit Team (AT) within five working days upon their approval as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely review and evaluation thereof; and 

(b) the Inspection and Acceptance Reports (IARs) within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance to the AT in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-010 to facilitate the inspection or validation of deliveries.
We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the persons liable to cause the settlement of the disallowances deemed final and executory.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the City Accountant to (a) prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the staled and cancelled checks; and (b) on a regular basis, reconcile the Cash-in-Bank balances with the records of the City Treasurer to ensure that all transactions affecting the cash accounts are captured in the CAO records.
The LCE informed that he instructed the CAO to implement immediately the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the LCE (a) issue designation to field collectors to act as an accountable or collecting officers; and (b) require the Manager of the CEEDO to (i) maintain a  logbook and file copies of RISs of issued accountable forms to control and monitor  the booklets of accountable forms issued to custodians; (ii) check that the field collectors are officially designated to perform collection functions and handle accountability for accountable forms; and (iii) verify regularly the statement of accountability for accountable forms of the custodian and each field collector as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the ending balances on hand for control and monitoring purposes.
In response to the audit recommendations, the CTO and the CEEDO in a letter submitted to the LCE commented that due to lack of plantilla position for collectors, the number of CTO collectors were augmented by CEEDO personnel.  To further strengthen the internal control in the handling of collections and accountable forms by the CEEDO, the CEEDO Manager and the CTO arrived at the following measures and adjustments:
a.	The CEEDO Manager shall issue an office memo designating a Custodian of Accountable Forms and LO in CEEDO; and
b.	The CTO shall issue an office memorandum strictly instructing the accountable officer to issue RIS to all collectors for all accountable forms received and issued by them.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) consider providing the CGSO and CAO with adequate manpower and training to  accomplish their tasks in the inventory taking, maintenance of PPELC, and reconciliation of PPE book balances and physical inventory report; (b) require the City General Services Officer, City Accountant, City Assessor, and City Legal Officer to coordinate their  efforts in the titling  and recognition in the books of  the parcels of land acquired by the CGM through purchase or donation based on their fair value or acquisition cost; and (c) instruct the City Accountant to (i) use the Road Networks and Water Supply System accounts in recognizing  the cost of the road network account on  road lots donated by private individuals; and (ii) recognize depreciation of PPE accounts in accordance with Section 262 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to require the (a) City Treasurer and the City Assessor to combine their efforts in cleansing the data base of the RPT System to generate a reliable assessment roll; (b) City Treasurer to furnish a list to the City Accountant of active RPT taxpayers and amount of collectibles to serve as basis in the setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year; and (c) City Accountant to (i) recognize in the books the amount of RPT/SET collectibles for the year based on the submitted list of RPT/SET receivables by the City Treasurer; and (ii) follow the prescribed accounting entries in the recording, distribution and payment of  the barangay’s share from the RPT income.

We recommended and the LCE is amenable to require (a) the City Accountant to (i) record the receipt of cash donations for calamity to account TL-DRRMF; (ii) maintain Registry of Appropriations for the DRRMF to monitor actual expenditures charged to the fund; (iii) transfer the remaining unexpended balance of QRF and MOOE for CYs 2019 and 2020 amounting to ₱12,087,034.41 and ₱5,363,639.20, respectively, to the TL-LDRRMF account; and (iv) revert to the GF the unexpended/unobligated  balance of CYs 2014 and 2015 DRRMF aged more than five years of ₱64,281,171.14; and (b) City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to (i) strictly comply with the submission of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team for timely verification thereof; and (ii) prepare the report in accordance with the format prescribed with emphasis on the inclusion of balances of previous years’ appropriation transferred to the STF and the utilization thereof.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) BAC to (i) consolidate the PPMPs into an APP; (ii) post the NOA, PO and contract in the website of GPPB and the PE, and any conspicuous place in the premises of the PE  for transparency purposes; and (iii) obtain price quotation/bids/final offers from at least three invited suppliers, in case of Negotiated Procurement for Emergency Cases; (b) City Accountant to (i) require contractors to submit the mandatory documentary requirements for procurement transactions; and (ii) prepare journal entry vouchers (JEVs) to correct the balance of Guaranty Deposits and total of Rent Expenses account; and (c) BAC, City General Service Officer and City Accountant to require contractors to submit the OSS, stipulate the exact specifications of goods purchased and include the terms and conditions in the contracts/PO.

We recommended and the LCE is amenable to require the (a) Supply/Property Officer under the CGSO to (i) take responsibility for the acceptance of all in-kind donations; (ii) collate all the acknowledgement receipts, proofs of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries; and (iii) take an inventory of remaining undistributed items and prepare a report thereon copy furnished the Audit Team; (b) City Accountant to (i) disclose the inventory of remaining undistributed items of in-kind donations in the Notes to Financial Statements; and (ii) record the procurement of goods under account Welfare Goods for Distribution and recognize as Welfare Goods Expenses or Donations only upon issuance of the inventories; and (c) City General Services Officer to submit to the CAO the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) accompanied by the RIS on a monthly basis for drawing a JEV to record the issuances made.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to require the (a) City Human Resources and Management Officer to submit a copy of the Minutes of Meeting directing the Department Heads to do the scheduling of employees who reported  to duty during the pandemic; (b) Department Heads to submit the schedule of employees required to work during the pandemic stating the dates and purpose as directed by the LCE; and (c) City Accountant to (i) correct the deficiencies noted in the  review of payrolls and attachments and effect corrections as needed, prior to payment; and (ii) submit  to the Audit Team the liquidation reports for the cash advances for the Hazard Pay totaling ₱15,269,530.78.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to require the City Accountant and City Treasurer to (a) submit to the Audit Team the liquidation reports for the cash advances for the SRA; and (b) City Accountant to (i) submit to the Audit Team the liquidation reports on all cash advances on a monthly basis and as soon as the purposes for which they were given have been served; and (ii) use the CDJ to record the disbursements made by the accountable officer.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) City Budget Officer and the CGM Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to  maximize the utilization of  idle Disaster Funds  under the  DRRMF before resorting to any augmentation of fund in order not to sacrifice the PPAs under the 20% DF; (b) BAC to indicate the required  technical specifications involving the acquisition of the desired type or nature of the goods in the POs and Contracts; and (c) City Accountant to(i) support with sufficient and appropriate documentation all transactions  prior to payment; (ii) adjust the balances of the accounts Advances to Officers and Employees and Advances to SDO to correct the misclassification of accounts in the recording of cash advances; (iii) cause the immediate submission of the unsubmitted paid DVs totaling to ₱1,920,585.25 to the Audit Team; and (iv) strictly adhere to the submission to the Audit Team of DVs and their supporting documents within ten days of the ensuing month.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) City of Malolos Development Council (CMDC) to set a realistic time-frame  for the  implementation of the identified priority development PPAs; and (b) BAC and City Engineer to (i) strictly comply with the  posting of information to notify the public of the on-going projects to promote transparency and accountability; and (ii) submit to the Audit Team the contracts and their supporting documents consisting of  the  legal and technical eligibility documents of the Contractors; (c) BAC to post for information purposes, the NOA, contract, PO including Notice to Proceed (NTP) if necessary, in the PhilGEPS website, the website of the Procuring Entity concerned, if available, and at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose; and (d) City Engineer to perform detailed engineering investigations, surveys and designs in  accordance with the standards and specifications prescribed by the  LCE prior to the  bidding and awarding of  any  contract for Infrastructure Projects.

12.7.	We recommended that the LCE require the (a) City Planning and Development Officer and City Budget Officer to ensure that the total LGU budget appropriations under the Annual Budget shall correspond to the activities supporting GAD PPAs; and (b) the GFPS (i) perform its  functions  as intended for gender mainstreaming in the CGM’s PPAs; (ii) strive for the full implementation of GAD-related PPAs by utilizing the whole amount set aside for the intended  purposes so that the desired outcomes are attained and benefits are enjoyed to the fullest by the intended beneficiaries; and (iii) conduct capacity development on gender mainstreaming, gender-responsive planning, budgeting, gender analysis and gender assessment, development  and utilization of GAD tools.
12.8.	Consistent with our audit recommendations, the LCE committed to coordinate closely with the various departments in addressing the various gender-related issues and streamlining GAD activities with the CGM’s plans and programs.

We recommended and the LCE is amendable to require (a) the City Accountant to adhere with the provisions of RA No. 7916 to protect the preferential rights of the contractors; (b) the Head of the MRF to monitor the quantity per cubic meter and weight of residual wastes hauled by contractors to ensure that payments made correspond to the services received; and (c) submit the Ten-year SWMP for approval to the NSWMC and subsequently, implement SWM PPAs as stated therein.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to (a) continuously observe the regulations and laws governing the withholding of taxes, premium contributions, etc. and accordingly improve the remittances of the same to the respective government agencies concerned, the BIR, GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG; and (b) establish coordination to facilitate processing of documents necessary to support the completeness of remittance, recording of transactions and timely transmission of DVs to the Offices concerned to facilitate the immediate remittance of the same to the concerned agencies.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to require the City Accountant to (a) adjust the distributed 30% share to barangays; and (b) prepare an ARPT Collection to support an accurate distribution of RPT shares to augment the scarce resources of the component small barangays.
The City Accountant committed to prepare adjustments on the succeeding payments to barangays.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the City Accountant to require (a) the concerned national government officials, members of the BAC, TWG and the consultant so submit the documentary requirements for the payments and refrain from paying honoraria with deficient documentation; and (b) recognize payments for honoraria to the consultant, students and madrasah instructor to their appropriate expense accounts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require (a) the City Accountant and City Treasurer to (i) submit the 8,658 DVs and their supporting documents to the  Audit Team; and  (ii) strictly comply with the timely rendition of accounts to the Audit Team so as not to delay the conduct of verification and post-audit activities; (b) City Accountant to  observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports such as Monthly Reports, Financial Statements and BRS to the Audit Team; (c) all concerned accountable officers to submit on time all financial documents and reports of  their respective transactions for each month to the CAO for the preparation of the financial reports;  and (d) impose sanctions against the accountable officers who continuously fail  to abide with the timely submission of documents and reports, as warranted.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the City General Services Officer to submit to the Audit Team (a) the approved contracts or POs and their supporting documents within five working days upon their approval to facilitate timely review and evaluation thereof; and (b) IARs within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance to facilitate the inspection or validation of deliveries.

19.5. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) persons liable to cause the settlement of the disallowances deemed final and executory; and (b) the City Accountant to (i) record in the books of accounts the issuance and settlements  of the disallowances and charges that have become final and executory as contained in the NFD; and (ii) maintain and properly update the subsidiary ledgers/records for each official/employee determined to be liable/responsible for the amount disallowed/charged/suspended.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE)

(a) require the AOs to strictly follow the prescribed liquidation period for cash advances to prevent the accumulation of outstanding cash advances;

(b) require the City Accountant to refrain from granting of multiple cash advances which practice exposes huge funds to possible loss; and

(c) instruct the concerned AOs to practice the proper way of recording transactions in the cashbook.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE :

(a) make representations with the BFP for the immediate remittance of the prior years’ unremitted 20% LGU share; and

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the

(a) CDRRMC to 

(i) prepare a comprehensive and detailed Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management  Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) that will address risk reduction and management system effectively to meet the needs for prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery of the Local Government; 

(ii) list the PPAs  specifically and  classify properly  the allotment  as either MOOE or CO; and 

(iii)  comply with the  prescribed format  which shall include the details of the projects charged to the previous years’ unexpended balance of the DRRMF;

(b) CDRRMO to strictly

(i) comply with the submission of monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Audit Team for timely verification and

(ii) prepare the report in accordance with the format prescribed under COA Circular 2012-002 with emphasis on the inclusion of balances of previous years’ appropriation transferred to the Special Trust Fund and the utilization thereof;

(i)follow  the reporting requirements for the DRRMF, specifically the proper transfer of previous year’s unexpended balances of the DRRMF to the Trust Fund; and

We recommended that the SP members stop the practice of giving assistance directly to indigents since this is not among their functions as a legislative or law-making body. We recommended that the LCE instruct the project manager and E-trike officers to fast track the implementation of E-Trike Project to avoid the revocation of the Deed of Donation and repossession of E-trikes by the DOE.

Coordination will be made with the GSO and other Offices concerned to facilitate the booking of the parcels of land       under the name of the City using their fair market value to be determined by the Appraisal Committee.

We recommended that the LCE require  the (a)  CTO and the City Assessor to combine their efforts to cleanse the data base of the RPT System to generate a reliable assessment roll; (b) CTO to provide a list to the CAO of active RPT taxpayers and amount collectible to serve as basis in the setting-up of RPT/SET Receivable at the beginning of the year, pending cleansing of Assessor’s records; and (c)  CAO, based on the submitted list of RPT/SET receivables by the CTO,                             recognize in the books the amount of RPT/SET collectibles for the year, and follow the prescribed accounting entries in the distribution and payment of barangay’s RPT share.
The LCE expressed that he will require the offices concerned to take action to ensure that realistic receivables from RPT and SET are set up in the books as target in the collection of RPT and SET.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the LCE (a) require the City Treasurer and City Accountant to (i) facilitate the return of the abandoned NALGU funds totaling ₱4,849,505.00 to the account of the National Treasury; and (ii) revert to the General Fund the cash donation amounting to ₱100,000.00, and other identified supposed income totaling ₱3,181,734.92;  (b) require the City Accountant to (i) retrieve the records, if possible, to effect the return of the unexpended balances of trust funds to source agencies; and (ii)  maintain a detailed subsidiary records  for trust funds for reference and proper identification; and (c) ensure that the 20% share from fire code fees is to be appropriated exclusively for the operations and maintenance of local fire station.
The LCE instructed the City Treasurer and the City Accountant to look into the   TFs and take necessary action to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the City Accountant (a) adjust the distributed 30% share to barangays in the first and second quarter of 2019; and (b) henceforth, prepare an Abstract of RPT Collection to support an accurate distribution of RPT shares to augment the scarce resources of the component small barangays.
The LCE agreed to implement the recommendations.

We recommended that the LCE make representations with the BFP (a) for the immediate remittance of the prior years’ unremitted 20% LGU share; and (b) to consider the outright remittance of the 20% LGU share from BFP-collected fire code fees in accordance with the R.A. No. 9514 and its IRR.

They further claimed that the funds will be readily available for release by the DBM upon submission by the City of liquidation report on the previously released funds.

We recommended  that the LCE as head of the CMDC, hand in hand with the CPDO, ensure that (a) the priority development projects are specifically identified in the AIP through thorough and regular discussion with other sectoral members of the CMDC; (b) the APP is  prepared for infrastructure projects; (c) a realistic time-frame is set for their implementation; and (d) public bidding as a mode of procurement is adopted for a smooth implementation of projects according to the Plan.
We also recommended that the BAC Secretariat and City Engineering Office take note of the deficiencies noted in the posting of signboard for information to the general public of ongoing project, and the requirements of the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184 be  religiously complied with  to ensure the promotion of transparency, competitiveness, integrity and quality of projects implemented by the City.
The LCE claimed that (a) the LGU already worked on identifying the specific projects to be undertaken to facilitate the smooth and continued implementation of the infrastructure projects in the ensuing year; and (b) collaboration with the CPDO and the City Engineering Office had been done to ensure a smooth implementation of the infrastructure project projects.

We recommended that the LCE (a) investigate the incurrence of negative slippage on several development projects, and take appropriate action to hold liable the City officials concerned, and contractors for the delay; (b) require the City Engineer to request from the contractors concerned a Statement of Work Accomplished, furnished the Audit Team; (c) require the BAC Secretariat (i) to forward to the Audit Team the lacking documents; and (ii) henceforth, submit the perfected contract together with its integral parts within five days from its signing; and (d) require the City Engineering Office (CEO) to (i) exercise regular monitoring of ongoing projects to enforce the completion of the projects on time; (ii) strictly observe the posting of billboards on ongoing projects for transparency and public information; and (iii) seek the assistance of the officials of the barangay concerned    to  keep an eye on the signboards to prevent them from being stolen. If warranted, impose penalty on takers of the signboard.
The LCE said that he will consider the audit recommendations.

We recommended  that the LCE support the advocacies of RA No. 9003 by (a) ensuring to make the City MRF fully functional and environmental friendly through established concrete and long-term programs, project and activities to promote waste reduction, recycling, composting, and waste processing in an environmentally sound manner; (b) utilizing the available machineries and keep them intact and in good condition to avoid manual processing of wastes; (c) continuing the education campaign on proper waste management and disposal to all component barangays; and (d) conducting regular meetings with component barangays, and different stakeholders to tackle the current status of the City's compliance with RA No. 9003 and come up with coordinated projects to address the common concerns on solid waste management.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) CDRRMC to (i) prepare a comprehensive and detailed Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management  Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) that will address risk reduction and management system effectively to meet the needs for prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery of the Local Government; (ii) list the PPAs  specifically and  classify properly  the allotment  as either MOOE or CO; and (iii)  comply with the  prescribed format  which shall include the details of the projects charged to the previous years’ unexpended balance of the DRRMF; (b) CDRRMO to strictly (i) comply with the submission of monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Audit Team for timely verification and (ii) prepare the report in accordance with the format prescribed under COA Circular 2012-002 with emphasis on the inclusion of balances of previous years’ appropriation transferred to the Special Trust Fund and the utilization thereof; (c) City Accountant to (i)follow  the reporting requirements for the DRRMF, specifically the proper transfer of previous year’s unexpended balances of the DRRMF to the Trust Fund; and (ii)  transfer only the expended balances of DRRMF for QRF and MOOE  to the Trust Liability account and  treat  the DRRMF for Capital Outlay as a continuing appropriation  in the General Fund books until the projects funded therefrom are completed; and (d) City Budget Officer to maintain separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for the DRRMF on the following: (i) RAAO - for Quick Response Fund; (ii) RAAO- for Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses; and (iii) RAAO- for Capital Outlay.
The LCE committed to implement the recommendations.

We recommended that the LCE require the CAO to evaluate the number and qualifications of its personnel and determine whether there is a need to improve the staffing pattern to cope with the preparation of the special accounts for the two economic enterprises.
The LCE said that he will consider the audit recommendations while the City Accountant agreed to maintain the Special Accounts.

We recommended that the LCE direct the City Social Welfare and Development Office to formulate measures/written policies/guidelines, for the approval of the Sangguniang Panglungsod, as to the qualification, kind/type of financial assistance, the circumstances and the amount of financial assistance to be given to requesting parties to maximize the utilization of the resources of the City thus benefiting a greater number of constituents, particularly those less privileged individuals.
