observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the (a) CTO to prepare the RCI with the date based on the actual issuance of check and submit to the CAO together with the paid DVs for preparation of the JEV;  (b) CAO to (i) prepare the JEV for the CkDJ based on the RCI submitted by the CTO to reflect the true cash in bank balances in the financial statements; (ii) immediately effect the adjustment of the long outstanding and stale checks, and improperly labeled deposit in transit in the books totaling ₱90,506.18 and ₱111,261.75, respectively, to present fairly the balance of the cash in bank account in the financial statements; (iii) make representation with the Postal Bank to verify the actual cash in bank balances maintained by the City; and (iv) henceforth, monitor closely the preparation of the BRS in order to effect the adjustments in the books, if any, in a timely fashion; and (c) require both Offices to conduct reconciliation of cash in bank records at least quarterly to identify the nature of differences and record the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
As per recommendation, both the CTO and CAO immediately reversed the process of check issuances by the CTO followed by the preparation of the JEV based on the RCI submitted for posting to the CKDJ.

The Management also instructed both Offices to conduct reconciliation of cash in bank at least every 3 months in order to determine and correct differences, if any.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management manifest commitment to resolve the aforementioned discrepancies and impose sound control over inventories by strict implementation of the foregoing provisions to attain sound internal control in the recording, management and issuance of supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: Management called the attention of the GSO to prepare and submit records of their inventory, PPE ledgers and stock card.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management (a) continue to be compliant with the terms and conditions of the City’s loans obligations; and (b) require the City Accountant to verify the loan difference of ₱2.90 with the records of the DBP, and effect the adjustment in the books, accordingly.

We recommended that the LCE require the City Accountant to (a) trace and review the details of the Due to NGA account balances with insufficient information, and effect the adjustment in the books on fund transfers erroneously recorded to Due to NGAs, and the appropriate dropping in the books of the destroyed motor vehicle that was compensated with insurance; (b) exert her best efforts in retrieving the financial records pertaining to the receipt and disbursements of the unexpended balance of trust funds totaling ₱4,395,176.49 in order to facilitate the return of funds to the National Treasury or source agency; (c) maintain a detailed subsidiary ledger and update the same promptly to avoid overdrafts and undetermined balances; (d) revisit the NGAS for proper use of accounts and recording of the flow of transactions; and (e) in coordination with the Local Finance Committee to assess the income of the City that were treated as trust liability to augment its local resources and ensure that those funds are to be appropriated for the delivery of basic services to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: There has been a communication between the CTO and the Bureau of Treasury instructing the former to deposit the dormant cash in bank to the National Treasury. The Management instructed the CAO to trace and review first the details of the unexpended balance of trust fund before making the necessary deposit to the National Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require (a) the Local Finance Committee and other concerned City officials in charge of preparing the budget to establish a realistic income forecast to ensure that the amount appropriated for the LDRRMF which is based on the estimated income from regular sources could be attained, and cash back up is assured, and (b) the City Accountant (i) in coordination with the City Budget Officer to revert the CY 2012 unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to unappropriated surplus of the GF; and (ii) henceforth, to ensure the recognition of correct accounting entries in the books for the transfer, disbursements, and closing of entries in the books affecting the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The Management has instructed the members of the finance committee to ensure that the budget allocation and income forecast is realistic and not bloated so that the LDRRMF and the budget for 2018 and 2019 can speak for itself.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the BAC and its Secretariat to (a) require the concerned suppliers to submit their class A legal documents to the Audit Team for further evaluation; (b) revisit the provisions of the 2016 Revised Rules of Procurement Law to ensure that (i) all procurements of the City shall pass through public bidding; (ii) in the event that procurement is done through alternative mode of procurement, make certain that the conditions to qualify as such are met, and supported with the approved BAC Resolution recommending alternative mode of procurement; and (iii) the City is entering into contract with reputable, qualified, and competent food suppliers by requiring them to submit their eligibility documents together with the license to operate and authority from the local health officials to sell food products/items; and (c) henceforth,  ensure that the provisions for posting of the RFQ, NOA and other documents on procurements worth ₱50,000.00 and above at the PhilGEPS are complied with for transparency, competitiveness, economy, and accountability.

We also recommended that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer ensure the completeness of the supporting documents of the DVs prior to payment.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned City officials to (a) strictly follow the rules and regulations set forth in the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 otherwise known as R.A. No. 9003, and E.O. No. 11, series of 2017, and monitor the implementation of various ordinances made by the Sangguniang Panglungsod in connection with solid waste management for strict enforcement; (b) reconstitute the SWMB to include representatives from a non-government organization, manufacturing industry, recycling industry, and concerned government agencies; (c) invest more on facilities and consider repairing the unserviceable equipment in order to promote agriculture, and composting to decrease the cost of hauling, and lessen the residual wastes transferred to the sanitary landfill; (d) provide the segregators and collectors with personal protective gears to protect them from the waste hazards; and (e) induce the cooperation of business owners as well as the public commuters in maintaining the cleanliness of the surroundings.

We recommended that the members of the LSB (a) refrain from charging expenditures from the SEF not authorized under the existing rules and regulation but rather develop high-impact and laudable program, projects, and activities consistent with the guidelines on the prioritization and utilization of SEF with complete regard to the Division’s annual improvement plan to ensure efficient and effective use of the fund; (b) based on the DepEd classroom deployment analysis, intensify the (i) construction of new classrooms giving consideration to the growing population of the public school students on account of the escalating cost of tuition fees in the private schools; and (ii) repairs and maintenance of school buildings to attain an environment conducive to learning and prevent the disruption of classes during rainy season without the need of the subsidy from the general fund; (c) submit a justification for the procurement of high-end equipment; and (d) exercise prudence in government spending by preventing the purchase of equipment that are considered extravagant and unnecessary.

We recommended that the LCE (a) coordinate with the members of the Sangguniang Panglungsod to expedite the enactment of an Ordinance that would update the provisions of the EERC particularly on the imposition of fees to sustain the increasing cost of maintaining an economic enterprise; (b) require the CAO to maintain special accounts for economic enterprise in the GF; and (c) require the CEEDO to (i) prepare the contract agreements with the market stalls’ occupants, renters of the MSCC,  space for promo booth, et.al. for transparency and control purposes; and (ii) ensure that collections from fixed stalls are issued with controlled official receipts and make certain that the collections are recorded in the registry of each occupant for monitoring purposes.

We recommended that the Management (a) create a TWG who shall be responsible in the monitoring and administration of the electronic collection system of the City; (b) require the CTO and the Systems Administrator to provide effective  controls to ensure that (i) access to data, software, equipment, and facilities are restricted to authorized individuals; (ii) ensure that incompatible  duties are separated; (iii) ensure that changes to system are authorized and systems are configured/operated securely as intended; and (iv) fully utilize the electronic collection system for market and other economic enterprise by requiring the CEEDO to submit the documents required for its full implementation; and (c) require the City Accounting Office to perform call-back procedures to match entries posted in the electronic collection system against manually prepared Report of Collection and Deposit (RCD) and official receipts to ensure completeness and accuracy of information.

We recommended that Management (a) update the implementing guidelines in the grant of educational assistance and ensure that it is approved and concurred by the members of the City Council; (b) formulate a policy guidelines in the processing, releasing and grant of medical and burial assistance parallel to the provisions of the DSWD MC to ensure that only those in  extreme need of financial assistance would be prioritized to the resources of the government; and (c) exercise prudence in the use of funds for donations and assistance by ensuring that only those requests that would give direct benefit to the public, especially to the poor, are qualified consistent with the guidelines of the DSWD MC.

We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and other Officials concerned (a) to submit the liquidation reports/report of disbursements for the financial assistance granted to the (i) chief of police for the peace and order program of the City for audit; (ii)  FSCAP for the monthly financial assistance intended for the procurement of medicines and giving of burial assistance to its members; and (b) henceforth, ensure that financial assistance granted to NGOs/CSOs and POs with specific intention should be recorded to appropriate Advances account for control and monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction:
The Management has drafted and submitted to the Sangguniang Panglungsod the revised implementing guidelines in the grant of educational assistance. A scholarship ordinance, specifically City Ordinance No. 41-2018, approved on June 14, 2018, was the result of such move.
The Management likewise has instructed the PNP, OSCA, FSCAP and all other NGOs and Pos to strictly adhere to the auditing guidelines in the liquidation of cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the CTO and the CAO to observe the timely remittance of GSIS, PHIC, and HDMF monthly contributions, and loan amortizations as prescribed by the existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management (a) submit legal basis for the payments to the PCL representing contributions of the councillors at ₱50,000.00 each; and (b) require the PCL to submit accomplishment reports on the projects funded by the City for further evaluation.
We recommended that the Management cause the settlement of the disallowance amounting to ₱90,000.00.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the CAO to (a) update and adjust the books relative to the (i) reversion to the Cash in Bank account and reversion to the payable or government equity the stale checks of ₱116,045.54; (ii) closing of the PB account and transfer of its cash balance to the LBP under the TF Proper; and (iii) recorded DBP account with reported balance of ₱15,760.35 but no records at the CTO;  (b) record in the books the deposit made to SEF account totaling ₱367,961.99; (c) record check disbursements based on the RCI submitted by the CTO. If unclaimed or unreleased checks were recorded in the books, indicate the same for adjustment of the cash in bank balance at year-end; and (d) henceforth, monitor closely the preparation of the BRS to effect any adjustments in the books in a timely fashion.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE (a) require the  CAO  to prepare an aging of cash advances for monitoring of cash advances and issue demand letters to accountable officers to compel them to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances;(b) refrain from granting cash advances to employees not designated as Special Disbursing Officers (SDOs);  (c) assess if there is a need to  increase the maximum cash accountability of DOs to cover the amount of their cash advances;  (d)  require the CAO to coordinate with the City Agriculture to (i) identify the grantees; (ii) monitor their payments; and (iii) recover the collectibles to sustain the program for other City constituents.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE (a)  require the CAO to recognize inventories as asset at the time of purchase, and expense based on the monthly issuance reports;(b) require the GSO to instruct the operating units such as the RHU and DepEd to prepare  the corresponding control documents such as Request Issuance Slip (RIS), Monthly reports of receipt and issue of  stocks; stocks cards and other property records for proper accountability and monitoring; (c) require the GSO to ensure  that all procurements/deliveries pass through rigid inspection and acceptance  prior to release to implementing units including that of the SEF-funded procurement; (d)  require the BAC and the GSO to comply with the posting requirement in the PhilGEPS and in three conspicuous places for small value procurement; and (e) comply with the required documentation of procurement of   drugs and medicines pursuant to  Food and Drug Administration (FDA) Act of 2009   and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the GSO, CAO, City Assessors Officer, and City Legal Office to further exert their coordinated efforts in ensuring that the parcels of land acquired by the City through purchase or donation are titled and recognized  in the books based on the fair value or acquisition cost to reflect the true financial condition of the City; (b) require the City Engineering Office to forward a copy of the inspection and acceptance documents on completed public infrastructure projects  for listing at the GSO ;  (c) ensure that the Offices of the GSO and CAO are adequately manned in order that said Offices could substantially accomplish their tasks in the PPE recording, documentation, monitoring, inventory taking, reporting, and reconciliation of records;  and (d) require the CAO to effect the necessary adjustments on the Land, Road Networks, depreciation, and derecognition of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the LCE require the CAO to (a) look closely on the discrepancies noted and effect the adjustment(s) to reflect the accurate balance of the Loans Payable account; and (b) submit copy of the JEV to the Audit Team effecting the adjustment(s) made on loans payable account together with supporting documents to verify the appropriateness of the entry(ies) made.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the CAO to (a) strictly follow the relevant rules and regulations prescribing the timely remittance of withheld Pag-IBIG, PhilHealth, and GSIS contributions; (b) explain the occurrence of discrepancies in the GL records vis-à-vis actual amount withheld and settle the discrepancies noted; and (c) closely monitor said accounts by conducting monthly reconciliation between the recognized withheld amounts and the remittances made.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the CAO to compute and withhold the appropriate taxes from the City’s contractors, suppliers and service providers and remit the same as required by the BIR; and (b) strictly follow the existing tax laws, rules and regulations to ensure the proper imposition and remittances of withholding taxes.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the CAO to (a) comply with the prescribed accounting entries in the transfer and utilization of the LDRRMF in accordance with PPSAS; (b)  record the unexpended balances of the CYs 2017 and 2018 to the TL- LDRRMF totaling ₱44,398,607.50; (c) revert the TL-LDRRMF, aged more than five years aggregating to ₱78,201,514.02  to the unappropriated surplus of the GF; and (d) adjust  the entry made for the amount transferred to GF amounting to ₱10,094,749.39 in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the LCE (a) revisit the terms and conditions of the MOA signed by and between the City and the DSWD to be properly guided by the obligations of the City in the implementation of the SFP; (b) open a separate trust fund account for the Supplemental Feeding Program; (c) require the CSWDO, GSO, CTO, CAO and the Parents –Teachers of the day care children to convene and evaluate the need to implement the SFP through cash advance to be used for the purchase of food items in accordance with the menu provided by the DSWD;  (d) require the GSO and the CSWDO to account for actual delivered items to day care centers by the Supplier prior to full payment;   (e) consider refraining from sourcing rice and food items from Suppliers other than the NFA; and (f) strictly abide by the laws, rules and regulations on posting of public information relating to PPA, pursuant to COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommended that the LSB chair (a) call for a frequent meeting with the members of the LSB to tackle the relevant and critical issues in the education system in the public sector, and in coordinated efforts, plan the allocation of the SEF largely on the priority projects enumerated under JC No. 01; (b) submit to the DepEd Regional Office the research output undertaken by the DepEd Division personnel as an input to the Department’s sharing and use of research results ; (c) set a budget limit for every activity and project of the DepED such as CLRAA, City Meet and Palarong Pambansa as contained in the project proposal approved by the authorized personnel of the LSB; (d) refrain from allowing the grant of cash advance without the supporting approved project proposal as a medium in determining how the cash advances are to be expended; and (e) submit an explanation on the unlocated LCD projectors aggregating to ₱799,000.00.

We recommended that the LCE require the City Treasurer to make a representation with the BFP to (a) request for the immediate remittance of the prior years’ unremitted 20% LGU share; and (b) consider requesting the outright remittance of the 20% LGU share from BFP-collected fire code fees in accordance with the R.A. No. 9514 and its IRR.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) reconstitute the CSWMB to include representatives from a non-government organization, manufacturing industry, recycling industry, and concerned government agencies; and monitor the implementation of various ordinances made by the Sangguniang Panglungsod in connection with solid waste management for strict enforcement.
We also recommended that the LCE as chairman of the CSWB (a) convene a meeting to tackle the current status of the City's compliance with R.A. 9003; (b) instruct the Human Resource Department to look into the lack of personnel at the CENRO for the latter to  properly perform and accomplish its functions; (c) fortify the "Abot Kamay" program by advocating that all Barangags undergo the lectures and workshops ; (d) require the concerned offices to report on the status of the Malolos Ecopark project, Material Recovery Facility, and the Solid Waste Management Plan and other existing programs/projects/activities on solid waste management;  (e) assign a point person that will facilitate the revisions needed in the ten-year Solid Waste Management Plan; and (f) discuss with the Board the adoption of waste reduction programs.

We recommended that the LDRRMC (a) monitor and review the progress and challenges in the implementation of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and to develop an elaborate action plan to ensure the fulfillment of programmed PPAs; (b) convene frequently or as necessary in order to formulate  programs, projects and activities for the previous years’ unexpended LDRRMF; (c) comply with the Disaster Preparedness Minimum Standards to strengthen the capacities to anticipate, cope and recover from negative impacts of any catastrophe; and (d) consider the construction of evacuation centers suitable for temporary habitation of the displaced families in times of calamities as required by RA No. 10121.
We also recommended that the City Accounting Office and City Budget Officer maintain the prescribed registries in the utilization of LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) convene the LDC hand in hand with the City Planning Development Office to identify specifically the development projects to be prioritized and implemented during the year to ensure timely implementation of projects; (b) create a monitoring team who shall be tasked to evaluate and monitor the implementation of planned projects; (c) require the City Engineer’s Office to (i) refrain from implementing projects “By Administration” in the absence of the City’s equipment in carrying out the project; (ii) submit to the Audit Team the perfected contract within five day from signing together with its integral parts for timely contract review; and (iii) observe the provision on full disclosure of implemented projects through appropriate posting of billboard/information.

We recommended that the LCE ensure that the CHO shall utilize the service vehicle exclusively for responding to constituents in need of emergency health service pending application at the DOH to convert the same to ambulance.
The City Health Officer assured the Audit Team that the Purchase Request for all drugs and medicines shall be certified by him as conforming to the PMDC of the PNDF.

We recommended that the LCE require the City Human Resource Management Officer: (a) prepare and issue a policy guideline on the screening and recruitment of JO personnel; (b) prepare a contract of service signed by and between the City and the JO detailing therein the works to be done, duration of the contract, rate per day, immediate supervisor, among others; (c)  require the JO to submit their quantified accomplishment report for the period signed by their immediate supervisor; and (d) refrain from assigning the JOs to sensitive functions such as custodian of funds, collecting functions, and assignments that would duplicate the works of regular employees.

We recommended that the LCE (a) continue its efforts in gender mainstreaming to achieve full compliance with the provisions of relevant laws and regulations especially on GAD Planning and Budgeting, and establishment and maintenance of GAD Database; and (b)require the GFPS to monitor and evaluate the implementation of the GAD programs, projects and activities against the GAD plan and budget  to ensure significant implementation of proposed activities

We recommended that the LCE (a) make a representation with the Sanggunian Panglungsod to enact ordinances to upgrade the LCR, GRPA, and Market Code to improve the City’s revenue performance and cope up with the rising cost of governance; (b) in the absence of the upgraded tax rates and regulatory fees, (i) set a realistic forecast of revenue based on the actual collections made in the previous years to avoid budget and cash overdraft; (ii) require the CEEDO and the City Treasurer’s Office to maintain  individual stallholders’ ledger cards wherein  rental payments are posted; and (iii) prepare monthly collection reports from market stallholders to ensure appropriate monitoring and accounting; and (c) require the City Accounting Office to maintain special accounts for market and other economic enterprises to determine their financial condition and results of operations.
We recommended that the LCE require the persons liable to cause the settlement of the disallowance amounting to ₱90,000.00.

We recommended that Management (a) require the concerned City officials and employee to immediately settle or liquidate their cash advances in accordance with the aforementioned rules and regulations; (b) refrain from granting cash advances to elected officials for unauthorized purposes; and (c) henceforth, require the City Accountant to prepare an ageing of cash advances on a regular basis for monitoring of cash advances, and immediate preparation of demand letter to erring accountable officers.
observations.managementAction: Management called the attention of the concerned CGM officials and employees to immediately settle or liquidate their cash advances in accordance with the prescribed rules and regulations. The granting of cash advances to elected officials was also disallowed except for travelling expenses. The City Accountant was also directed to monitor the cash advances, its grant and liquidation on a regular basis.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) create (a) a special team that shall undertake the (i) inventory of fixed and non-fixed assets of the City; (ii) organization of the property cards and equipment/real property cards of the GSO and Accounting Office, respectively; and (iii) reconciliation of the discrepancies on non-fixed assets amounting to ₱231,260,943.91 between the Accounting and GSO records, and the land account of ₱54,721,756.47; and (b) a disposal committee to undertake the inventory, appraisal and disposal of unserviceable PPE to prevent further deterioration and loss of value of the unserviceable PPE.

We also recommended that the City Accountant effect the adjustments in the books on the purchased assets that were not recognized to appropriate PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the City GSO to furnish the CAO with the Inventory of PPE for proper reconciliation. As regards the inventory, appraisal and disposal of unserviceable equipment, the Management formed a disposal committee to undertake the disposal of such to prevent further deterioration and loss of value.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE (a) ensure that the infrastructure projects included in the CDP for 2017-2019 aim to direct the economic and social development of the City and be implemented efficiently and effectively by ensuring that the PPAs presented in the AIP are sourced from the CDP of the City; (b) ensure that the 20% DF is utilized in accordance with the existing guidelines; and (c) have the BAC, Engineering Office, and concerned officials to re-visit and review the process in the procurement of infrastructure projects, and ascertain the propriety of the documents supporting the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Management has instructed the Budget Office and the City Planning Office to ensure that all infrastructure projects included in the CDP be included in the process flow from the crafting of the AIP to itemizing PPA to the budget preparation so that the efficiency and effectiveness of the project be always realized for the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE thru the Head of the LDRRMO to (a) take heed on the areas identified in the GAR that require/need improvements to ensure full preparedness at the strike of disasters; (b) prepare a reliable AIP for LDRRMP to ensure that all projects to be implemented and funded by the LDRRMF are included thereat; (c) refrain from charging unauthorized expenses from the LDRRMF; (d) require the City Treasurer, City Budget Office, and the City Accounting Office to coordinate with each other in monitoring the budget collections vis-à-vis actual collections to ensure the availability of funds to avoid payment/obligation of expenditures with no cash back up; and (d) require the City Budget Office to prepare separate registries for the LDRRMF’s QRF, MOOE, and Capital Outlay.

We recommended that Management (a) initiate capacity building programs on GAD especially for the members of the GFPS to strengthen their knowledge and be able to perform their duties productively; (b) plan the establishment of a centralized GAD database to help in identifying emerging gender issues; and (c) create a separate GAD office that will support and coordinate the initiatives of the GFPS and for proper monitoring and evaluation of all GAD related PPA's.

We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC TWG and end-users to observe the preparation of PPMP and APP to ensure the attainment of efficient and effective procurement process through competitive bidding. When justified by circumstances, the conditions and requirements of alternative mode of procurement shall be strictly adhered in accordance with rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: At the start of the FY 2017 and start of the FY 2018, the Management is already implementing the policy on the preparation of the PPMP and APP of all departments and offices. All PPAs not included in the PPMP and APP are outrightly denied. The implementation started in the 2nd quarter of FY 2017 and 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Health Officer, the Rural Health Physicians, and the BAC follow strictly the provisions of the existing rules and regulations in the procurement of drugs and medicines, and other medical supplies to ensure the safety and health of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Management has instructed the CHO, thru the Local Health Board (LHB), to ensure that the procurement process and its rules and regulations is strictly followed. The Management also reminded the LHB to ensure that the supply of medicine be always available and of good quality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management (a) require the members of the LCPC to take concerted effort to properly align their tasks with their planned PPAs; (b) reconstitute the LCPC to include representatives from private and youth sector to produce an effective and efficient PPAs to address the child issues; and (c) conduct regular meeting to tackle about possible actions to mitigate various child risks and set clear-cut guidelines for the implementation of both ECCD and LCPC programs and activities..

We recommended that the LCE require the CTO to (a) strictly adhere with the provisions cited above in the preparation of the CRAAF to accurately report the actual issued accountable forms and the balances on hand as of the given period; and (b) demand officially the remittance of collections and the copy of the CTCs issued in prior years, from the previous Barangay administration.

We commended the Management for its action of ensuring that the City is compensated or indemnified from loss or damage due to fire, earthquake, flood and other unforeseen events, however, we also recommend that General Services Office to include the MSCC and other determined insurable properties for insurance to the GSIS. We further recommended that the City continuously comply with the R.A.
observations.managementAction: The Management sincerely appreciated the Audit Team for commending City for substantially complying with R.A. No. 656.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management hold the concerned City officials liable for the payments of irregular expenditures totaling ₱540,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The Management claimed that the private organization provides free education to those less-privileged but deserving students. However, as recommended, the City has stopped the payment of rental fee.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to:

a)	strictly adhere with the  regulations  on the grant and liquidation of the Cash advances for Confidential Fund   particularly on the submission  to the concerned COA Audit Team Leader (COA ATL) for post audit of the cash advance voucher with covering transmittal letter  enumerating the supporting documents; and

b)	record the liquidation in the books within five days after the receipt of the copy of the transmittal of documents duly acknowledged by the ICFAU and to  submit to the COA ATL  the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher recording the liquidation of the Cash Advance.
observations.managementAction: The City officials committed to adhere with the regulations in the release and liquidation of cash advances for CIF. The concerned City officials submitted to the audit team the disbursement vouchers for the cash advances of CIF on January 29, 2018.
observations.reason: The management assured the Audit Team to submit to COA Central Office the liquidation of the cash advances for CIF immediately.

observations.managementAction:
The GSO has conducted the inventory of the PPE for CY 2017.


The GSO has exerted efforts in starting the maintenance of property cards. However, the proper filling up of the property cards was not made in accordance to the existing rules and regulations.
observations.reason:
Absence of initiative on the part of the concerned City officials. Reiterated in Observation No. 04 of the 2017 AAR

Lack of manpower. Again, the City Accountant stated to comply with the recommendation

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that 

a.) the City’s Finance Committee and Local School Board ensure that the estimated collectible income and projected expenses are realistic and reasonable and 



b.) Management should exert its best effort in improving collection efficiency in order to meet the targets and eliminate the deficit incurred.
observations.managementAction:
The City officials committed to be more cautious in the disbursement of funds to have a significantly improved cash position this coming year. However, for the audit year 2017, again, the estimate income was not achieved.


According to the City Treasurer, regular tax mapping and campaign are being conducted by CTO.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 07 of the 2017 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board revisit the laws, rules and regulations covering the use of the Special Education Fund and ensure compliance therewith.
observations.managementAction: According to the concerned LSB officials, the DepEd has sufficient program for the construction of school building hence they did not prioritize the same project from the school board budget as it will only cause overlapping of the same project.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 15 of the 2017 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned officials of the City Government submit copies of perfected contracts, POs, JOs, notices of deliveries and inspection reports to the audit team within the prescribed period together with the supporting documents pursuant to the aforementioned regulations.
observations.managementAction: We were informed by the City Administrator that he will initiate meeting with the concerned Offices in order to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Based on observation, one of the reasons for non-implementation of the recommendation is the failure of the Management to define the specific duties and responsibilities of each Office involved in the procurement, and keeping and distribution/submission of documents to concerned Offices.

observations.managementAction: Management informed the Audit Team that instructions were given to BAC to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 11 of the 2017 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee of the City Government adhere strictly with the procurement law particularly in resorting to the alternative modes of procurement and to conduct a thorough evaluation of the eligibility requirements and qualifications of prospective suppliers and contractors before the award of procurement contracts/ Purchase Orders should be made.
observations.managementAction: Management informed the Audit Team that instructions were given to BAC to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 11 of the 2017 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

(a) Management formulate clear policies/guidelines in the granting of financial assistance and to implement stricter control/supervision over the evaluation process to ensure that only qualified beneficiaries are granted assistance; and

(b) that officials and employees responsible for the transactions in question should be held liable.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with the recommendations
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 20 of the 2017 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

(a) the City Government review its computation of the honoraria being paid to the members of the BAC, TWG and BAC secretariat to strictly comply with the DBM guidelines; and

 (b) require the concerned personnel to return the excess amount paid to them.
observations.managementAction: The City Administrator committed to effect the refund of excess payments of honoraria through salary deduction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City include in its priorities the formulation and implementation of a Gender and Development (GAD) Code and to carefully plan programs and activities that are in line with the goals/objectives of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The CSWDO committed to adhere with the GAD regulations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 10 of the 2017 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the (a) CTO to prepare the RCI with the date based on the actual issuance of check and submit to the CAO together with the paid DVs for preparation of the JEV;  (b) CAO to (i) prepare the JEV for the CkDJ based on the RCI submitted by the CTO to reflect the true cash in bank balances in the financial statements; (ii) immediately effect the adjustment of the long outstanding and stale checks, and improperly labeled deposit in transit in the books totaling ₱90,506.18 and ₱111,261.75, respectively, to present fairly the balance of the cash in bank account in the financial statements; (iii) make representation with the Postal Bank to verify the actual cash in bank balances maintained by the City; and (iv) henceforth, monitor closely the preparation of the BRS in order to effect the adjustments in the books, if any, in a timely fashion; and (c) require both Offices to conduct reconciliation of cash in bank records at least quarterly to identify the nature of differences and record the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management (a) require the concerned City officials and employee to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances in accordance with the aforementioned rules and regulations in the grant and liquidation of cash advances; (b) refrain from granting cash advances to elected officials for unauthorized purposes; and (c) henceforth, require the City Accountant to prepare an ageing of cash advances on a regular basis for monitoring of cash advances, and immediate preparation of demand letter to erring accountable officers.

We recommended that Management manifest commitment to resolve the aforementioned discrepancies and impose sound control over inventories by strict implementation of the foregoing provisions to attain sound internal control in the recording, management and issuance of supplies and materials.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) create (a) a special team that shall undertake the (i) inventory of fixed and non-fixed assets of the City; (ii) organization of the property cards and equipment/real property cards of the GSO and Accounting Office, respectively; and (iii) reconciliation of the discrepancies on non-fixed assets amounting to ₱231,260,943.91 between the Accounting and GSO records, and the land account of ₱54,721,756.47; and (b) a disposal committee to undertake the inventory, appraisal and disposal of unserviceable PPE to prevent further deterioration and loss of value of the unserviceable PPE.
We also recommended that the City Accountant effect the adjustments in the books on the purchased assets that were not recognized to appropriate PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Management (a) continue to be compliant with the terms and conditions of the City’s loans obligations; and (b) require the City Accountant to verify the loan difference of ₱2.90 with the records of the DBP, and effect the adjustment in the books, accordingly.

We recommended that the LCE require the City Accountant to (i) trace and review the details of the Due to NGA account balances with insufficient information, and effect the adjustment in the books on fund transfers erroneously recorded to Due to NGAs, and the appropriate dropping in the books of the destroyed motor vehicle that was compensated with insurance; (ii) exert her best efforts in retrieving the financial records pertaining to the receipt and disbursements of the unexpended balance of trust funds totaling ₱4,395,176.49 in order to facilitate the return of funds to the National Treasury or source agency; (iii) maintain a detailed subsidiary ledger and update the same promptly to avoid overdrafts and undetermined balances; (iv) revisit the NGAS for proper use of accounts and recording of the flow of transactions; and (v) in coordination with the Local Finance Committee to assess the income of the City that were treated as trust liability to augment its local resources and ensure that those funds are to be appropriated for the delivery of basic services to the constituents.

We recommended that the LCE require (a) the Local Finance Committee and other concerned City officials in charge of preparing the budget to establish a realistic income forecast to ensure that the amount appropriated for the LDRRMF which is based on the estimated income from regular sources could be attained, and cash back up is assured; (b) the City Accountant (i) in coordination with the City Budget Officer to revert the CY 2012 unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to unappropriated surplus of the GF; and (ii) henceforth, to ensure the recognition of correct accounting entries in the books for the transfer, disbursements, and closing of entries in the books affecting the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the LCE (a) ensure that the infrastructure projects included in the CDP for 2017-2019 aim to direct the economic and social development of the City and be implemented efficiently and effectively by ensuring that the PPAs presented in the AIP are sourced from the CDP of the City; (b) ensure that the 20% DF is utilized in accordance with the existing guidelines; and (c) have the BAC, Engineering Office, and concerned officials to re-visit and review the process in the procurement of infrastructure projects, and ascertain the propriety of the documents supporting the transactions.

We recommended that the LCE thru the Head of the LDRRMO to (a) take heed on the areas identified in the GAR that require/need improvements to ensure full preparedness at the strike of disasters; (b) prepare a reliable AIP for LDRRMP to ensure that all projects to be implemented and funded by the LDRRMF are included thereat; (c) refrain from charging unauthorized expenses from the LDRRMF; (d) require the City Treasurer, City Budget Office, and the City Accounting Office to coordinate with each other in monitoring the budget collections vis-à-vis actual collections to ensure the availability of funds to avoid payment/obligation of expenditures with no cash back up; and (d) require the City Budget Office to prepare separate registries for the LDRRMF’s QRF, MOOE, and Capital Outlay.

We recommended that Management (a) initiate capacity building programs on GAD especially for the members of the GFPS to strengthen their knowledge and be able to perform their duties productively; (b) plan the establishment of a centralized GAD database to help in identifying emerging gender issues; and (c) create a separate GAD office that will support and coordinate the initiatives of the GFPS and for proper monitoring and evaluation of all GAD related PPA's.

We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC TWG and end-users to observe the preparation of PPMP and APP to ensure the attainment of efficient and effective procurement process through competitive bidding. When justified by circumstances, the conditions and requirements of alternative mode of procurement shall be strictly adhered in accordance with rules and regulations.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC and its Secretariat to (a) require the concerned suppliers to submit their class A legal documents to the Audit Team for further evaluation; (b) revisit the provisions of the 2016 Revised Rules of Procurement Law to ensure that (i) all procurements of the City shall pass through public bidding; (ii) in the event that procurement is done through alternative mode of procurement, make certain that the conditions to qualify as such are met, and supported with the approved BAC Resolution recommending alternative mode of procurement; and (iii) the City is entering into contract with reputable, qualified, and competent food suppliers by requiring them to submit their eligibility documents together with the license to operate and authority from the local health officials to sell food products/items; and (c) henceforth,  ensure that the provisions for posting of the RFQ, NOA and other documents on procurements worth ₱50,000.00 and above at the PhilGEPS are complied with for transparency, competitiveness, economy, and accountability.
We also recommended that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer ensure the completeness of the supporting documents of the DVs prior to payment.

We recommended that the City Health Officer, the Rural Health Physicians, and the BAC follow strictly the provisions of the existing rules and regulations in the procurement of drugs and medicines, and other medical supplies to ensure the safety and health of the constituents.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned City officials to (a) strictly follow the rules and regulations set forth in the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 otherwise known as R.A. No. 9003, and E.O. No. 11, series of 2017, and monitor the implementation of various ordinances made by the Sangguniang Panglungsod in connection with solid waste management for strict enforcement; (b) reconstitute the SWMB to include representatives from a non-government organization, manufacturing industry, recycling industry, and concerned government agencies; (c) invest more on facilities and consider repairing the unserviceable equipment in order to promote agriculture, and composting to decrease the cost of hauling, and lessen the residual wastes transferred to the sanitary landfill; and (d) provide the segregators and collectors with personal protective gears to protect them from the waste hazards; and (f) induce the cooperation of business owners as well as the public commuters in maintaining the cleanliness of the surroundings.

We recommended that the members of the LSB (a) refrain from charging expenditures from the SEF not authorized under the existing rules and regulation but rather develop high-impact and laudable program, projects, and activities consistent with the guidelines on the prioritization and utilization of SEF with complete regard to the Division’s annual improvement plan to ensure efficient and effective use of the fund; (b) based on the DepEd classroom deployment analysis, intensify the (i) construction of new classrooms giving consideration to the growing population of the public school students on account of the escalating cost of tuition fees in the private schools; and (ii) repairs and maintenance of school buildings to attain an environment conducive to learning and prevent the disruption of classes during rainy season without the need of the subsidy from the general fund; (c) submit a justification for the procurement of high-end equipment; and (d) exercise prudence in government spending by preventing the purchase of equipment that are considered extravagant and unnecessary.

We recommended the Management (a) require the members of the LCPC to take concerted effort to properly align their tasks with their planned PPAs; (b) reconstitute the LCPC to include representatives from private and youth sector to produce an effective and efficient PPAs to address the child issues; and (c) conduct regular meeting to tackle about possible actions to mitigate various child risks and set clear-cut guidelines for the implementation of both ECCD and LCPC programs and activities.

We recommended that the LCE (a) coordinate with the members of the Sangguniang Panglungsod to expedite the enactment of an Ordinance that would update the provisions of the EERC particularly on the imposition of fees to sustain the increasing cost of maintaining an economic enterprise; (b) require the CAO to maintain special accounts for economic enterprise in the GF; and (c) require the CEEDO to (i) prepare the contract agreements with the market stalls’ occupants, renters of the MSCC,  space for promo booth, et.al. for transparency and control purposes; and (ii) ensure that collections from fixed stalls are issued with controlled official receipts and make certain that the collections are recorded in the registry of each occupant for monitoring purposes.

We recommended that the LCE require the CTO to (a) strictly adhere with the provisions cited above in the preparation of the CRAAF to accurately report the actual issued accountable forms and the balances on hand as of the given period; and (b) demand officially the remittance of collections and the copy of the CTCs issued in prior years, from the previous Barangay administration.

We recommended that the Management (a) create a TWG who shall be responsible in the monitoring and administration of the electronic collection system of the City; (b) require the CTO and the Systems Administrator to provide effective  controls to ensure that (i) access to data, software, equipment, and facilities are restricted to authorized individuals; (ii) ensure that incompatible  duties are separated; (iii) ensure that changes to system are authorized and systems are configured/operated securely as intended; and (iv) fully utilize the electronic collection system for market and other economic enterprise by requiring the CEEDO to submit the documents required for its full implementation; and (c) require the City Accounting Office to perform call-back procedures to match entries posted in the electronic collection system against manually prepared Report of Collection and Deposit (RCD) and official receipts to ensure completeness and accuracy of information.

We recommended that Management (a) update the implementing guidelines in the grant of educational assistance and ensure that it is approved and concurred by the members of the City Council; (b) formulate a policy guidelines in the processing, releasing and grant of medical and burial assistance parallel to the provisions of the DSWD MC to ensure that only those in  extreme need of financial assistance would be prioritized to the resources of the government; and (c) exercise prudence in the use of funds for donations and assistance by ensuring that only those requests that would give direct benefit to the public, especially to the poor, are qualified consistent with the guidelines of the DSWD MC.
We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and other Officials concerned (a) to submit the liquidation reports/report of disbursements for the financial assistance granted to the (i) chief of police for the peace and order program of the City for audit; (ii)  FSCAP for the monthly financial assistance intended for the procurement of medicines and giving of burial assistance to its members; and (b) henceforth, ensure that financial assistance granted to NGOs/CSOs and POs with specific intention should be recorded to appropriate Advances account for control and monitoring purposes.

We commended the Management for its action of ensuring that the City is compensated or indemnified from loss or damage due to fire, earthquake, flood and other unforeseen events, however, we also recommend that General Services Office to include the MSCC and other determined insurable properties for insurance to the GSIS. We further recommended that the City continuously comply with the R.A.

We recommended that the LCE require the CTO and the CAO to observe the timely remittance of GSIS, PHIC, and HDMF monthly contributions, and loan amortizations as prescribed by the existing rules and regulations.
We recommended that the Management hold the concerned City officials liable for the payments of irregular expenditures totaling ₱540,000.00.

We recommended that the Management (a) submit legal basis for the payments to the PCL representing contributions of the councillors at ₱50,000.00 each; and (b) require the PCL to submit accomplishment reports on the projects funded by the City for further evaluation.
We recommended that the Management cause the settlement of the disallowance amounting to ₱90,000.00, and the prior years’ disallowance of ₱300,549.00.
We recommended that the City government remit all the RPT collections due to all the barangays and strictly adhere with the regulations pertaining thereto.
We recommended that the City Government strictly comply with the regulation governing the repair and maintenance of government motor vehicles and equipment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 

(a)  see to it that the granting of financial assistance be made only in accordance with the very purpose and objectives thereof  as provided by existing laws and regulations;  and  

(b) the City Budget Officer and the other officers of the City who certified and signed   the transactions be held liable for the refund of the total amount  of the transactions without prejudice to the filing of the appropriate administrative and or criminal charges against them.

We recommended that the City Government comply strictly with the law and regulations governing the Local Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Fund and the transfer of the unexpended balance of the fund, which should be duly supported with the cash.

observations.recommendation:
1.5     We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to:
a) strictly adhere with the  regulations  on the grant and liquidation of the Cash advances for Confidential Fund   particularly on the submission  to the concerned COA Audit Team Leader (COA ATL) for post audit of the cash advance voucher with covering transmittal letter  enumerating the supporting documents; and
b) record the liquidation in the books within five days after the receipt of the copy of the transmittal of documents duly acknowledged by the ICFAU and to  submit to the COA ATL  the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher recording the liquidation of the Cash Advance.
2.5  	We recommended that the City government remit all the RPT collections due to all the barangays and strictly adhere with the regulations pertaining thereto.

3.2	We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (a) create inventory committee to facilitate the completion of the inventory taking for all their properties and the preparation and submission of the corresponding report thereof, (b) require the City Accountant and the CGSO to maintain and update their respective property records and regularly reconcile them, (c) create disposal committee to take appropriate action for the disposition of unserviceable properties and (d) require the City Accountant to prepare the necessary lapsing schedule to support the computation for depreciation for all their depreciable properties.

4.10	We recommended that a.) the City’s Finance Committee and Local School Board ensure that the estimated collectible income and projected expenses are realistic and reasonable and b.) Management should exert its best effort in improving collection efficiency in order to meet the targets and eliminate the deficit incurred.
5.4	We recommended that the Local School Board revisit the laws, rules and regulations covering the use of the Special Education Fund and ensure compliance therewith.

6.6	We recommended that the concerned officials of the City Government submit copies of perfected contracts, POs, JOs, notices of deliveries and inspection reports to the audit team within the prescribed period together with the supporting documents  pursuant to the aforementioned regulations.

7.16	We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee and other concerned officials and personnel of the City (a) strictly comply with the Government Procurement Reform Act or the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and Section 7(a) of RA No. 6713; (b) refrain from granting food assistance to unqualified beneficiaries and  resorting to  unnecessary spending of government funds and; (c) desist from making expenditures that are not supported by available appropriations.

8.7	We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee of the City Government adhere strictly with the procurement law particularly in resorting to the alternative modes of procurement and to conduct a thorough evaluation of the eligibility requirements and qualifications of prospective suppliers and contractors before the award of procurement contracts/ Purchase Orders should be made.
9.6	We recommended that the City Government strictly comply with the regulation governing the repair and maintenance of government motor vehicles and equipment.

10.6 	We recommended that (a) Management formulate clear policies/guidelines in the granting of financial assistance and to implement stricter control/supervision over the evaluation process to ensure that only qualified beneficiaries are granted assistance; and (b) that officials and employees responsible for the transactions in question should be held liable.

11.17	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a)  see to it that the granting of financial assistance be made only in accordance with the very purpose and objectives thereof  as provided by existing laws and regulations;  and  (b) the City Budget Officer and the other officers of the City who certified and signed   the transactions be held liable for the refund of the total amount  of the transactions without prejudice to the filing of the appropriate administrative and or criminal charges against them.

12.3	We recommended that the City Government stop from granting honoraria without legal basis and specific authority and require the concerned Barangay Captains to refund the total honorarium given to them during the year.

13.7	We recommended that (a) the City Government review its computation of the honoraria being paid to the members of the BAC, TWG and BAC secretariat to strictly comply with the DBM guidelines; and (b) require the concerned personnel to return the excess amount paid to them.

14.5	We recommended that the City Government comply strictly with the law and regulations governing the Local Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Fund and the transfer of the unexpended balance of the fund, which should be  duly supported with the  cash back up.

15.5	We recommended that the City include in its priorities the formulation and implementation of a Gender and Development (GAD) Code and to carefully plan programs and activities that are in line with the goals/objectives of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01.
16.4	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the full settlement of disallowed payments of ₱820,227.93 for CY 2014 and the prior years’ disallowances of ₱300,549.90.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 
(a) continue to practice restraint and prudence in the disbursement of funds, giving priority to highly necessary, high-impact projects, programs and activities (PPAs); and 

(b) identify and implement revenue-enhancing strategies to further improve  collection efficiency.
observations.managementAction: The City officials committed to be more cautious in the disbursement of funds to have a significantly improved cash position this coming year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management 

(a) direct the City Treasurer and the City Assessor to coordinate closely in the preparation of property assessment and tax records for purposes of computing taxes due; 

(b) require the City Treasurer to furnish the City Accountant at the beginning of the year a duly certified list showing names of taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible for the year; and 

(c) instruct the City Accountant to set up in the books the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year instead of at year-end to produce more accurate financial reports.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management 
(a) require the City Accountant to review the details of the account and require the execution of promissory notes from each borrower as a reminder that repayment of the principal has to be made in due time; 

(b) consider the request for write off for long dormant accounts particularly those that are more than ten years; and 

(c) set up the necessary allowance for impairment for the said receivable as required under  the PPSAS.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive establish control mechanisms and standard operating procedures in the operation, accounting for the collections and management of the MSSC  with a view of making the facility into a self-sustaining economic enterprise.

We recommended that Management 

(a) continue to invest in high-impact development projects in the utilization of its 20% development fund pursuant to the intent of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 11, 2011 and

(b) improve further the City’s projects implementation to attain a one hundred per cent accomplishment rate thru improved planning and efficient procurement process to facilitate project implementation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) continue to be fully compliant with the waste segregation policy at source; and

(b) continue to embark on effective projects and programs that support the City’s ecological solid waste management program to sustain economic development that is in harmony with the environment.

We recommended that Management observe strict compliance with BIR regulations on the proper withholding and remittance of taxes to help the national government in the mobilization of funds to finance the national government’s projects.

We recommended that Management inform the Accounting Office and Treasury Office to keep up their being responsible in complying with the requirements of the GSIS in the deduction of GSIS Premium for SIF and ECIF and the timely remittances thereof.
